schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 8ac8e69d37fb638c75738981eb5cdf14c2687698
findings:
  - { id: judged-dcj-guard-structural-agreement, kind: judged, text: "dc-1 dispatches --preflight BEFORE the CI-only/--force-local publish guard (close.go:113-120), so a preflight run from a plain local checkout can report ready while a real close from the identical checkout still refuses at that guard. Parent closure-ergonomics dc-5 claims the preflight report and close's refusal are 'one code path' so their required agreement (co-3) is 'structural'. For this specific divergence the agreement is NOT structural: it is restored by a printed disclosure (the same boolean predicate called from a second print site). The story self-caught this twice (judged-dcj-2 and the re-swept follow-on) and closed it in prose, but unlike the dc-2 render-split, dc-3 widening, and dc-4 excluded-record tensions, no ADJ-33 hand-disposition is cited for this guard-bypass gap against dc-5's structural-agreement wording, and no supersedes/exempts edge exists (ADJ-26 forbids them this round). Residual undeclared tension: dc-5's structural claim is weakened to a shared-predicate convention at this one seam without an explicit adjudication ratifying that weakening. (confidence 0.30)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "Controller adjudication ADJ-34 (2026-07-16), no-conflict: parent dc-5's 'one code path / structural agreement' claim binds the closure-gate VERDICT layer, where agreement is structural via the shared evaluation functions. The CI publish guard is an environmental execution precondition, not a closure condition; its seam shares the identical predicate (lint.ReadCIEnv().InCI + the --force-local flag, never reimplemented) invoked from a second print site, and ac-1's mandatory disclosure clause fires whenever the guard would bite - so co-3's never-first-disclosure property holds for every refusal close can produce, the guard included. The weakening-to-a-shared-predicate-convention the judge names IS the structural sharing dc-5 requires: one predicate, two call sites, zero drift surface. Preflight exit 0 with the guard disclosure is honest - the verdict speaks to closure conditions, the disclosure speaks to the environment.", target_ref: spec/closure-ergonomics }

Parent closure-ergonomics dc-5 claims the preflight report and close's refusal are 'one code path' so their required agreement (co-3) is 'structural'. For this specific divergence the agreement is NOT structural: it is restored by a printed disclosure (the same boolean predicate called from a second print site). The story self-caught this twice (judged-dcj-2 and the re-swept follow-on) and closed it in prose, but unlike the dc-2 render-split, dc-3 widening, and dc-4 excluded-record tensions, no ADJ-33 hand-disposition is cited for this guard-bypass gap against dc-5's structural-agreement wording, and no supersedes/exempts edge exists (ADJ-26 forbids them this round). ---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-dcj-guard-structural-agreement** [no-conflict]: dc-1 dispatches --preflight BEFORE the CI-only/--force-local publish guard (close.go:113-120), so a preflight run from a plain local checkout can report ready while a real close from the identical checkout still refuses at that guard. Parent closure-ergonomics dc-5 claims the preflight report and close's refusal are 'one code path' so their required agreement (co-3) is 'structural'. For this specific divergence the agreement is NOT structural: it is restored by a printed disclosure (the same boolean predicate called from a second print site). The story self-caught this twice (judged-dcj-2 and the re-swept follow-on) and closed it in prose, but unlike the dc-2 render-split, dc-3 widening, and dc-4 excluded-record tensions, no ADJ-33 hand-disposition is cited for this guard-bypass gap against dc-5's structural-agreement wording, and no supersedes/exempts edge exists (ADJ-26 forbids them this round). Residual undeclared tension: dc-5's structural claim is weakened to a shared-predicate convention at this one seam without an explicit adjudication ratifying that weakening. (confidence 0.30) — Controller adjudication ADJ-34 (2026-07-16), no-conflict: parent dc-5's 'one code path / structural agreement' claim binds the closure-gate VERDICT layer, where agreement is structural via the shared evaluation functions. The CI publish guard is an environmental execution precondition, not a closure condition; its seam shares the identical predicate (lint.ReadCIEnv().InCI + the --force-local flag, never reimplemented) invoked from a second print site, and ac-1's mandatory disclosure clause fires whenever the guard would bite - so co-3's never-first-disclosure property holds for every refusal close can produce, the guard included. The weakening-to-a-shared-predicate-convention the judge names IS the structural sharing dc-5 requires: one predicate, two call sites, zero drift surface. Preflight exit 0 with the guard disclosure is honest - the verdict speaks to closure conditions, the disclosure speaks to the environment.
